chapter_title.ar: باب الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ دُخُولِ الْمَسْجِدِ وَ الْخُرُوجِ مِنْهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 19 – The words (to be spoken) during entering the Masjid and exiting from it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/96)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/96)
text: قَالَ الْفَضْلُ فِي دُخُولِ الْمَسْجِدِ أَنْ تَبْدَأَ بِرِجْلِكَ الْيُمْنَى إِذَا دَخَلْتَ وَ بِالْيُسْرَى إِذَا خَرَجْتَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Salih Bin Saeed Al Rashidy, from Yunus,

(It has been narrated) from them<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The superior act during entering the Masjid is that you begin with your right foot when you enter, and with your left foot when you exit’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Salih ibn Sa‘id al-Rashidiy from Yunus who has said the following: “’A’immah, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, have said, ‘When entering a Masjid, it is better to step in with the right foot and to step out with left foot when leaving a Masjid.”
